design_philosophy.core_loop: Realistic flight operation: pre-flight checks, navigation planning, aircraft control, landing procedures. Player manages multiple systems simultaneously (engines, avionics, aerodynamics).
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Fidelity vs. accessibility. The simulator prioritizes aerodynamic accuracy (blade element theory) over simplified arcade mechanics, creating tension between realism and playability.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Become a competent pilot. Experience authentic cockpit operations and physics-based flight dynamics without decades of actual training.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: X-Plane is uncompromisingly committed to flight physics realism. It models actual aerodynamic principles rather than simplified approximations. This creates a simulator designed for 'serious flight simulator enthusiasts' rather than casual players. The philosophy rejects hand-holding; learning the systems is the player's responsibility.

difficulty.checkpointing: Free flight mode allows pause and reset. Failure results in crash/restart from last saved state.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Complete agency. Players can adjust aircraft complexity, weather conditions, failure scenarios, and autopilot assistance. Difficulty scales from simplified trainer aircraft to complex systems-heavy variants. Players essentially set their own challenge curve.

progression.upgrade_system: Aircraft unlocks and add-on packs. Players can purchase or mod additional aircraft types and scenery. No traditional progression gates.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Optional; players start with a base fleet and expand through DLC or community add-ons. No mandatory progression tree.
replayability_hooks: Aircraft diversity (simple trainers to complex airliners)

world_building_techniques: Dynamic weather tied to real meteorological data
